name: booking-template-jetty-fix-2026-06-16
description: Fix HTTP ERROR 400 Ambiguous URI Path Separator in booking template functionality caused by Jetty 11.x to 12.x upgrade

entries.summary: Jira findings: PDSUPPORT-203319 and ION-16032 - Template names with forward slash (/) cause HTTP 400 Bad Request
entries.detail:
PDSUPPORT-203319: User saijai.yamsri@dsv.com cannot open booking templates with slash in name.
Sample failing template names:
- "Indorama Polyester to MXZLO /HAPAG"
- "TAURUS MANUFACTURING TO AUMEL_MELBOURNE, AUSTRALIA/ANL"
Error: 400 Bad Request in network console.

ION-16032: Engineering ticket for this defect.

Root cause: TemplateResource uses @Path("/{name}") where template name is a @PathParam.
When template name contains /, the URL-encoded %2F is rejected by Jetty 12's strict UriCompliance (AMBIGUOUS_PATH_SEPARATOR violation).
Jetty version: 12.1.9, Dropwizard version: 5.0.1.

Dropwizard 5.0.1's HttpConnectorFactory has getUriCompliance()/setUriCompliance() methods confirmed via javap.
UriCompliance.Violation.AMBIGUOUS_PATH_SEPARATOR confirmed in Jetty 12.1.9.
UriCompliance has with() method to create custom compliance from DEFAULT.
Using Claude Opus 4.6 (model ID: claude-opus-4.6) via GitHub Copilot CLI

entries.summary: Decision: Fix via UriCompliance config + @Path regex change. Both are needed.
entries.detail:
Fix approach (2 changes needed):
1. JETTY CONFIG: Configure UriCompliance via postSetupHook in BookingApplication to allow AMBIGUOUS_PATH_SEPARATOR.
   Use DefaultServerFactory.getApplicationConnectors() to set UriCompliance on each HttpConnectorFactory.
   Code: UriCompliance.DEFAULT.with("BOOKING_TEMPLATE", UriCompliance.Violation.AMBIGUOUS_PATH_SEPARATOR)

2. JAX-RS ROUTING: Change @Path("/{name}") to @Path("/{name: .+}") on TemplateResource methods.
   Reason: When %2F is decoded to / by the servlet API, JAX-RS routing treats / as path separator.
   {name: .+} regex allows matching across path separators.

Security: Only AMBIGUOUS_PATH_SEPARATOR is allowed (not LEGACY/UNSAFE). Path traversal protection remains intact because we're not allowing AMBIGUOUS_PATH_SEGMENT or AMBIGUOUS_PATH_ENCODING.

API compatibility: Preserved - same URL structure, same path parameter name. The regex just widens what the path param can match.

entries.summary: Task complete: 2 commits on branch, all tests pass (unit + integration)
entries.detail:
Task complete. Branch defect/ION-16032-booking-template-issue has 2 commits:
1. 786befc2e8 - Failing test (path annotation regex validation + parameterized tests)
2. 61355a98e4 - Fix (UriCompliance config + @Path regex change)

All tests pass: mvn test -pl booking (unit) and mvn verify -pl booking (integration).

Changes:
- TemplateResource.java: @Path("/{name}") → @Path("/{name: .+}") on GET, PUT, DELETE methods
- BookingApplication.java: Added configureUriCompliance() postSetupHook setting UriCompliance.DEFAULT.with(AMBIGUOUS_PATH_SEPARATOR) on all HTTP connectors
- TemplateResourceTest.java: Added @Nested WhenTemplateNameContainsSpecialCharacters with 3 path annotation tests + parameterized tests for special chars

entries.summary: Claude critical review of ION-16032 fix: approach correct, but test coverage does not actually exercise the bug
entries.detail:
Reviewed branch code + tests. Fix approach (UriCompliance.with(AMBIGUOUS_PATH_SEPARATOR) + @Path {name: .+}) is correct and Option A is the right choice. Goal of no-impact to existing API and non-slash template names is met for runtime behavior.

GAPS:
1) [HIGH] Tests give false confidence. Parameterized tests call templateResource.getTemplate(...) directly as a Java method - bypasses Jetty/Jersey routing & URI decoding entirely; they pass even with fix reverted (.+ vs [^/]+ is invisible to a direct method call). configureUriCompliance() has ZERO coverage - the Jetty-layer half that actually fixes the 400 is unverified, including the security claim that only AMBIGUOUS_PATH_SEPARATOR is relaxed. The 3 *_pathAnnotation reflection tests ARE a legit guard against the annotation regressing to {name} (fallback [^/]+ fails the match). No DropwizardAppExtension/ResourceExtension/JerseyTest exists anywhere under booking/src/test. Recommend an HTTP-level test: GET /booking/template/<name%2Fslash> -> assert 200 (not 400/404) + negative test that %2e%2e%2f still rejected; plus a unit test on configureUriCompliance.
2) [MED] UriCompliance relaxation is connector-wide (applied to all application+admin connectors), not template-scoped. Doc says 'no functional impact on existing endpoints' - true for routing (other endpoints keep [^/]+, so encoded slash now 404s at Jersey instead of 400 at Jetty) but understates that the app-wide encoded-slash posture changed. Per-connector is the only option in Jetty so app-wide is acceptable; doc should state the real scope.
3) [LOW] configureUriCompliance no-ops silently if server factory is not DefaultServerFactory - add LOGGER.warn in else branch.
4) [NIT] Doc shows with('BOOKING_TEMPLATE') but code uses with('BOOKING') - align.

Routing correctness verified OK: GET /template still hits listTemplates; non-slash names route identically under .+ and [^/]+; path traversal still blocked (Jetty normalizes .., AMBIGUOUS_PATH_SEGMENT/ENCODING still enforced, service lookups are actor-scoped DynamoDB keys).

Findings written to booking/docs/2026-06-16-booking-template-jetty-issue.md under 'Claude Critical Review'.

entries.summary: Implemented all 4 critical-review recommendations; committed fd8ef97d74. Booking unit suite green (2019 tests, 0 fail).
entries.detail:
Commit fd8ef97d74 on defect/ION-16032-booking-template-issue (3 files, doc kept untracked per request).

Finding 1 (HIGH) closed without adding dropwizard-testing (no in-memory Jersey harness exists in booking; pom carefully manages Jackson conflicts - too risky). Used only on-classpath libs:
- NEW BookingApplicationUriComplianceTest: asserts bookingUriCompliance() relaxes EXACTLY AMBIGUOUS_PATH_SEPARATOR on top of DEFAULT, keeps AMBIGUOUS_PATH_SEGMENT/ENCODING/PARAMETER/EMPTY_SEGMENT disallowed, DEFAULT baseline does not allow it, and applyUriCompliance wires it onto every application+admin HttpConnectorFactory.
- NEW TemplateResourceTest.JaxRsRoutingMatchesDecodedSlashNames: drives decoded literal-slash path through Jersey's real org.glassfish.jersey.uri.UriTemplate using live @Path annotations. Production {name: .+} matches+captures full slash name; pre-fix {name} ([^/]+) does NOT match (reproduces routing bug, fails on regression); non-slash names identical under both.
These fail in CI if either half of the fix is reverted - the gap the review flagged.

Finding 2 (MED) closed: doc states relaxation is connector-wide; other endpoints keep [^/]+ so encoded slash -> 404 at Jersey not 400 at Jetty; added Javadoc note.
Finding 3 (LOW) closed: configureUriCompliance now LOGGER.warn (naming factory class) on non-DefaultServerFactory instead of silent no-op; refactored into bookingUriCompliance()/applyUriCompliance() (behaviour unchanged).
Finding 4 (NIT) closed: doc label aligned to code with('BOOKING').

Verification: mvn test -pl booking = 2019 tests, 0 failures/0 errors. Targeted run of the two changed classes = 49 tests pass.
